data.change_summary: Addressed all 4 pending amendments. (1) Removed the dead `_started_at: DateTime<Utc>` parameter from `find_matching_running_task_run` (model.rs:462) and `matching_finalized_task_runs` (model.rs:479) since both functions match solely by exact run_id. Updated all call sites in fs.rs and the model.rs test. (2) Added a backward-compatibility documentation block on `collapse_task_run_attempts` (model.rs:499) explaining that the legacy runless matching pipeline (`find_collapse_group_index` → `unique_open_legacy_group_index` → `legacy_group_accepts_completion`) is a read-only path deliberately retained for grouping old ndjson rows written before run_id became required. (3) Enhanced the multi-open-match error in `update_task_run` (fs.rs, the `_` arm of `open_matches`) to include the mismatched run_id(s) of all open entries in the error message, improving debuggability when multiple open entries exist with different run_ids. (4) Added `fail_legacy_runless_entries` upgrade repair helper that marks all running entries with `run_id: None` as failed with 'superseded: run_id is now required (upgrade repair)'. Called at the start of both `record_task_run_start` and `update_task_run` after reading entries, ensuring mid-flight legacy milestones from older code versions are cleanly failed rather than stranded or producing confusing errors.

steps.description: Amendment 1: Removed dead `_started_at: DateTime<Utc>` parameter from `find_matching_running_task_run` (model.rs:462) and `matching_finalized_task_runs` (model.rs:479). Updated fs.rs call sites (record_task_run_start matching_finalized_task_runs call and find_matching_running_task_run call) and the model.rs test `matching_finalized_task_runs_ignores_different_named_run_at_same_started_at`.

Amendment 2: Added backward-compatibility documentation block on `collapse_task_run_attempts` (model.rs:499) explaining the legacy runless matching pipeline is a read-only path retained for old data — no new rows are written without run_id since the write paths require `run_id: &str` at the port boundary.

Amendment 3: Enhanced the multi-open-match `_` arm error in `update_task_run` (fs.rs) to collect and include the run_id(s) of all open entries: 'ambiguous task run update for bead=X project=Y run=Z; open runs: [run-A, run-B]'. This helps operators identify which entries are conflicting when multiple open entries exist with different run_ids.

Amendment 4: Added `fail_legacy_runless_entries` helper on FsTaskRunLineageStore that scans for running entries with `run_id: None` and marks them as Failed with outcome_detail 'superseded: run_id is now required (upgrade repair)' and finished_at set to the current operation timestamp.
Amendment 4: Integrated `fail_legacy_runless_entries` at the start of `record_task_run_start` (after reading entries, before collapse/matching) — writes the repaired entries if any were modified.
Amendment 4: Integrated `fail_legacy_runless_entries` at the start of `update_task_run` (after reading entries, before empty check and matching) — writes the repaired entries if any were modified.
Ran cargo test --lib (329 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy -- -D warnings (clean), cargo fmt --check (clean).

validation_evidence: cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors
grep confirms _started_at no longer appears in find_matching_running_task_run or matching_finalized_task_runs signatures

collapse_task_run_attempts doc comment block explicitly documents: 'read-only backward-compat path' for legacy runless matching, 'No new rows are written without run_id — the write paths in record_task_run_start and update_task_run require run_id: &str at the port boundary'
Multi-open-match error now includes 'open runs: [run-A, run-B, ...]' listing the mismatched run_ids of all open entries

fail_legacy_runless_entries marks running entries with run_id=None as Failed with descriptive outcome_detail and finished_at, called from both record_task_run_start and update_task_run before any matching logic

Upgrade repair is idempotent: entries already terminal are skipped (the is_terminal() check), and once repaired the entries have run_id=None + terminal outcome which won't be matched by exact run_id filters

All 329 prior tests continue to pass — no existing test creates entries with run_id=None via the service API (they all go through record_bead_start which requires run_id: &str), so the repair has no visible effect on test data
